summary: Markets surged to fresh records Friday as Intel's blockbuster Q1 earnings reignited the AI chip rally and the Justice Department dropped its criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. The S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7,165 and the Nasdaq jumped 1.6% to 24,837, both closing at all-time highs, while Intel soared 23% after crushing estimates and raising guidance on explosive data-center CPU demand. The DOJ's decision to refer the Powell probe to the Fed's Inspector General removes a cloud over Fed leadership ahead of Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearings, though Powell has not confirmed whether he'll step down in May or remain as governor. Crypto held steady with Bitcoin around $78,100 and Ethereum near $2,350, supported by ETF inflows despite ongoing Iran war jitters. Geopolitical optimism also lifted risk appetite after reports that Iran's foreign minister would meet Pakistani mediators for potential U.S. peace talks, extending the week's rally. The big macro story remains AI-driven earnings strength—Wall Street analysts now expect S&P 500 earnings to grow 18% in 2026—offset by elevated oil prices and consumer sentiment at record lows.

signals.title: Intel's Q1 earnings beat triggers semiconductor surge to record highs
signals.desc: Intel posted Q1 EPS of $0.29 vs. estimates and raised Q2 guidance, driving the stock up 23% to a new all-time high and lifting Nvidia past $5 trillion market cap as AI chip demand accelerates.

signals.title: DOJ drops Powell criminal probe, clearing path for Fed leadership transition
signals.desc: The Justice Department referred the investigation into Fed headquarters renovation overruns to the Fed's Inspector General, removing a key obstacle to Kevin Warsh's confirmation and boosting market confidence in Fed independence.

signals.title: Bitcoin consolidates near $78,000 as volatility cools and traders unwind leverage
signals.desc: BTC trades in a tight range with open interest dropping, signaling cooling momentum while institutional ETF demand provides support despite geopolitical uncertainty around Iran talks.

signals.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit record highs on Iran peace talk optimism
signals.desc: Reports that Iran's foreign minister will meet Pakistani mediators in Islamabad raised hopes for renewed U.S.-Iran negotiations, extending the April rally and lifting risk appetite across equities.

signals.title: Software stocks tumble as sector rotation into semiconductors intensifies
signals.desc: IBM and ServiceNow fell sharply after earnings, signaling investor preference for AI infrastructure plays over legacy software despite broad market strength.

verdict: Risk-on sentiment dominates as AI earnings momentum and geopolitical détente overshadow inflation concerns, positioning equities for further upside into next week's mega-cap tech earnings.
